title: salix pulchra subsp. pulchra
intro: Salix pulchra subsp. pulchra, also known as the white willow, is a subspecies of the willow plant that is native to Alaska and Canada. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, that can help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The health benefits of Salix pulchra subsp. pulchra include its ability to alleviate headaches, fever, and rheumatism, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues. Salix pulchra subsp. pulchra can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, to be used for medicinal purposes.
